summary: The available literature does not contain any direct studies evaluating whether continuous low-dose IV epinephrine affects uterine blood flow specifically in pregnant ewes. Although epinephrine may act via β₂ receptors to relax smooth muscle and reduce contraction frequency—potentially improving uteroplacental circulation—the possibility of unintended vasoconstriction cannot be ruled out without empirical support; therefore, no definitive conclusions can be drawn based solely on current knowledge.
